verses.1: “Do you know the time when the mountain goats give birth?
verses.2: Can you count the months that they fulfill?
verses.3: They bow themselves. They bear their young.
verses.5: “Who has set the wild donkey free?
verses.6: whose home I have made the wilderness,
verses.7: He scorns the tumult of the city,
verses.8: The range of the mountains is his pasture.
verses.9: “Will the wild ox be content to serve you?
verses.10: Can you hold the wild ox in the furrow with his harness?
verses.11: Will you trust him, because his strength is great?
verses.12: Will you confide in him, that he will bring home your seed,
verses.13: “The wings of the ostrich wave proudly,
verses.14: For she leaves her eggs on the earth,
verses.15: and forgets that the foot may crush them,
verses.16: She deals harshly with her young ones, as if they were not hers.
verses.17: because God has deprived her of wisdom,
verses.18: When she lifts up herself on high,
verses.20: Have you made him to leap as a locust?
verses.21: He paws in the valley, and rejoices in his strength.
verses.22: He mocks at fear, and is not dismayed,
verses.24: He eats up the ground with fierceness and rage,
verses.25: As often as the trumpet sounds he snorts, ‘Aha!’
verses.26: “Is it by your wisdom that the hawk soars,
verses.27: Is it at your command that the eagle mounts up,
verses.28: On the cliff he dwells and makes his home,
verses.29: From there he spies out the prey.
verses.30: His young ones also suck up blood.
